items.title: Emergency warning issued after blaze at Perth battery recycling facility

children.content: A man has been taken to hospital and a HAZMAT emergency warning has been issued as a serious fire burns at a lithium-ion battery recycling facility in Perth.

items.title: WA considering separate stockpile of diesel amid fuel shortages

image.altText: A hand on a diesel pump at a petrol station.

children.content: The WA government is considering creating its own "strategic stockpile" of diesel, saying the state's size and the reliance of the agriculture and resources industries on the fuel warrants a separate reserve.

items.title: Calls for greater protections for Rottnest after Indigenous remains discovered

children.content: Aboriginal leaders call for greater protections for Rottnest Island after the discovery of Indigenous remains during construction work at the popular holiday resort last week.

image.altText: An aerial photo of the Bathurst Lighthouse in front of a series of premium tents at Rottnest Island.

items.title: Firefighter encouraging more women to join front line

children.content: Beelerup firefighter Kahlia Lloyd says joining the brigade was the most empowering thing she had done.

image.altText: Woman looking at camera standing in front of fire truck.

items.title: Caravan park residents evicted after change of ownership

children.content: Reg Hoyling thought his park home in Albany was where he would spend the rest of his life, but a sale of the caravan park meant he was among the tenants evicted.

items.title: 'They're not just big fluffy puppy dogs': Breeder warning over Highland cattle popularity

children.content: Registered Highland cattle breeders have more than tripled in Western Australia in the past five years. The shaggy Scottish breed is gaining popularity across WA, with more breeders interested in taking on the cattle and strong demand from agritourism.

items.title: The Aussie diver, the religious influencer and a 42m death dive

children.content: Vali stands atop a cliff. He screams, psyching himself up. Three, two, one jump — then in mere seconds he smacks the water 42 metres below, sustaining massive injuries.

image.altText: A shirtless man falling sideways through the air.

items.title: Saints make blistering start against Power in Gather Round finale

children.content: Gather Round wraps up at Adelaide Oval, with the Power facing off with the Saints. Follow live.

image.altText: Max Hall of the Saints chased by Darcy Byrne-Jones of the Power at AFL's Gather Round.

items.title: Four dead after fatal crashes on WA roads

children.content: Four people have died on WA roads after two separate crashes in the space of just over 15 hours.

items.title: Ban on live sheep trade has another knock-on effect on WA wool

children.content: Elders is relocating its Western Australian wool handling and marketing operations to Victoria in response to declining wool production volumes in WA.

items.title: 'This was not easy': Artemis II crew's first public comments after lunar mission

children.content: Artemis crew members have described their mission as "the greatest dream on Earth" after landing on Saturday and setting a new deep-space travel record.

articles.title: Why did we go back to the Moon, 50 years after Apollo?

children.content: In both 1968 and 2026, a mission to the Moon offered a refreshing blast of positivity for a conflict-ridden world. So what's different now — and what do we really gain from this new moonshot, five decades after the first?

image.altText: Two side by side images of a moon landscape on the bottom and a partial Earth circle in the top right corners as seen in space.

articles.title: How NASA and the Artemis II crew completed the 'perfect' splashdown

children.content: After nine days, one hour, 32 minutes and 15 seconds in space — and with a host of new discoveries and a rapt Earth watching — four astronauts journeyed farther from Earth than any human before them.

image.altText: Three red and white parachutes hover above the sea, hanging from them is a cone-shaped spacecraft

items.title: Plans to move residents off Cocos Islands walked back after backlash

children.content: After seething criticism from residents, the Commonwealth has released its final plan to protect the Cocos (Keeling) Islands from rising sea levels.

image.altText: White surf crashes against a seawall of stacked sand bags, with tropical plants and coconut trees in the background.

items.title: UK man jailed for abusing girl may face renewed visa cancellation

children.content: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

image.altText: A swing at an Adelaide playground site.

items.title: Debutant scores as Matildas kick off FIFA Series with thumping win

children.content: Leticia McKenna nets a goal on her international debut, as the Matildas beat Malawi 5-0 to start their FIFA Series in Kenya.

image.altText: Sam Kerr and Kaitlyn Torpey run to celebrate a goal in a Matildas game against Malawi in Nairobi, Kenya.

items.title: Bones found on Rottnest Island are Indigenous remains

children.content: Several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island this week are historic Indigenous remains, WA Police say.

image.altText: Police holding guard around a fence

items.title: Man who killed foster brother to stay in jail due to lack of mental facilities

children.content: Ian Brooke was found not guilty of murder due to his mental impairment, but will have to stay in jail because of a lack of suitable mental health care facilities to house him.

items.title: A budget 'leak' so good, the journalist's editors didn't believe it

children.content: It was "the best Canberra scoop in years", but the journalist's own editors did not believe it, and almost buried the story.

items.title: Is sitting with your legs crossed actually bad for you?

children.content: The healthiest sitting position is often the one you do not hold for the next hour.

items.title: Minister casts doubt on road user tax due to EV uptake concerns

children.content: A new tax on road users, including electric vehicles, has been cast into doubt by the transport minister, who says now may not be the time for it.

items.title: The biggest group of kids at risk of falling through NDIS cracks

children.content: With just months to go until Thriving Kids starts, parents and experts are warning a particular cohort of children is at risk of falling through the cracks.

children.content: A television show that uses generative artificial intelligence to animate Indonesian folktales is creating a stir.

items.title: McIlroy 'unravels' at Masters as chasers catch him in third round

children.content: It will not be a procession to a second straight Masters title for Rory McIlroy, after a tense third round at Augusta National.

image.altText: Rory McIlroy walks the Augusta National course during the third round of the Masters.

items.title: Gout Gout smashes his Australian 200m record and goes under 20 seconds at Nationals

children.content: Gout Gout has produced a breathtaking run to break 20 seconds in the 200 metres for the first time, and smash his own national record.

image.altText: Sprinter Gout Gout beats his chest and screams with joy

items.title: I thought I understood China. Then I ordered a craft beer

children.content: On a midsummer night last year, I took a sip of lager at a craft beer pub in my hometown in China's south. But this was not just a beer. It was a cultural symbol that shows just how much China's Gen Z is changing.

gallery.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols
